note:
Facebook和Twitter周三采取行动，限制“纽约邮报”(New York Post)报道的分发，未经证实的说法涉及民主党总统候选人乔·拜登(Joe Biden)，导致特朗普总统的竞选团队和盟友指控这些公司存在审查制度。
两家社交媒体公司都表示，这些举措旨在减缓潜在虚假信息的传播。但他们几乎没有透露他们是如何做出决定的细节，这引发了人们对他们应用规则缺乏清晰度和一致性的批评。
“纽约邮报”周三刊登了一系列报道，援引据称是拜登的儿子亨特发送的电子邮件，新闻媒体称这些邮件是从特朗普的私人律师鲁迪·朱利安尼和特朗普的前顾问史蒂夫·班农那里收到的。
Facebook发言人安迪·斯通(Andy Stone)表示，Facebook正在限制“华盛顿邮报”主要内容的分发，而其外部事实核查人员则对这些说法进行了审查。这意味着该平台的算法不会将链接到这篇报道的帖子放在人们的新闻馈送中如此高的位置，从而减少了观看它的用户数量。然而，根据Facebook旗下研究工具CrowdTangle的数据，这个故事在Facebook上仍被点赞、分享或评论了近60万次。
斯通说，Facebook有时会采取这一步骤，如果它看到一些获得吸引力的信号是虚假的，以便让事实核查人员有时间在故事广泛传播之前对其进行评估。他没有透露Facebook使用什么信号或采取这种方法的频率的更多细节。
推特走得更远。发言人特伦顿·肯尼迪(Trenton Kennedy)表示，该公司禁止用户发布这些电子邮件的图片或“纽约邮报”(New York Post)两篇涉及这些邮件的报道的链接，并援引其规则，禁止分享通过黑客攻击获得的包含私人信息的内容。“纽约邮报”发言人特伦顿·肯尼迪(Trenton Kennedy)表示，该公司禁止分享通过黑客攻击获得的包含私人信息的内容。
尝试在Twitter上共享链接的用户会看到一则通知，上面写着：“我们无法完成此请求，因为此链接已被Twitter或我们的合作伙伴识别为具有潜在危害。”
如果用户点击已经发布在Twitter上的链接，用户将被带到一个警告屏幕，上面写着：此链接可能不安全，他们必须单击该链接才能阅读文章。推特还要求《纽约邮报》删除关于这个故事的推文。
推特表示，之所以决定屏蔽这些链接，是因为它无法确定这些电子邮件的来源。该公司表示，其政策禁止使用我们的服务分发未经授权获得的内容，并表示不想通过允许人们分享可能是非法获得的材料来鼓励黑客攻击。
但该公司拒绝评论它是如何做出这一决定的，也拒绝评论它对“华盛顿邮报”报道中的电子邮件有哪些证据进行了权衡。
该公司的安全团队在一条推文中表示，这些文章中的电子邮件图像包括个人和私人信息-比如电子邮件地址和电话号码-这违反了我们禁止未经授权分享此类细节的规定。
首席执行官杰克·多尔西(Jack Dorsey)承认，该公司关于为何屏蔽这些文章的沟通不是很好。他在Twitter上发帖称，阻止人们在没有任何上下文的情况下分享我们为什么屏蔽这些文章是不可接受的。
当被问及对社交网络行动的评论时，“纽约邮报”发言人伊娃·本森提到了NPR新闻的一篇文章，这篇文章是由该报编辑委员会撰写的。
社论说，我们的故事解释了信息的来源，参议院的一个委员会现在证实，他们也从同一个来源收到了这些文件。然而，Facebook和Twitter却故意阻止用户阅读并自行决定这是什么意思。
最近几周，Twitter和Facebook采取了更积极的行动，以遏制与选举相关的虚假声明和操纵行为的蔓延，这是避免2016年的情况重演的努力的一部分，当时与俄罗斯有关联的演员利用社交媒体瞄准美国选民。
Facebook一直在警告黑客和泄密操作的可能性，即被盗文件或其他敏感材料被策略性泄露-就像2016年民主党全国委员会(Democratic National Committee)和希拉里·克林顿(Hillary Clinton)竞选团队被黑客攻击的电子邮件所发生的那样。
但这些公司周三的举措招致了一些专家的批评，他们表示，Facebook和Twitter需要更清楚地解释他们的政策，以及他们实施这些政策的频率。
研究在线言论监管的哈佛法学院(Harvard Law School)讲师伊夫林·杜克(Evelyn Douek)表示：这个故事是未来几周我们可以预期会发生的事情的一个缩影，我认为它展示了为什么平台拥有它们准备坚持的明确政策真的很重要。
她说，目前还不清楚他们是否在这种情况下异常介入，如果有，为什么会这样做。这不可避免地会引发我们所看到的那种强烈抗议，那就是他们这么做是出于政治原因，因为他们是有偏见的。
共和党人抓住这一事件作为他们长期以来声称社交网络审查保守派声音的证据。没有统计证据支持这些说法。
特朗普在推特上说，这太可怕了，Facebook和Twitter删除了这篇报道，尽管Facebook没有将其从其平台上删除。他写道，废除第230条，他指的是一个长期存在的法律保护，保护在线平台不会因为人们发布的帖子而被起诉，并表示他们不会因为合理限制这些帖子而受到惩罚。特朗普多次呼吁废除第230条。
周三，密苏里州参议员乔希·霍利(Josh Hawley)致函Facebook和Twitter的首席执行官，敦促他们做出减少分发和屏蔽这篇报道的决定。
霍利还给联邦选举委员会写了一封信，称这些公司的行为可能构成了令人震惊的竞选资金违规行为，使拜登的竞选活动受益。
由怀俄明州共和党参议员约翰·巴拉索(John Barrasso)领导的参议院共和党会议在推特上写道，“再见，@Jack，”并配以“华盛顿邮报”报道的图片。Twitter首席执行官杰克·多尔西(Jack Dorsey)定于10月28日-距离大选只有六天-与Facebook首席执行官马克·扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)和谷歌首席执行官桑达尔·皮查伊(Sundar Pichai)一起在参议院商务委员会作证。主题：第230节。
note_en:
Facebook and Twitter took action on Wednesday to limit the distribution of  New York Post reporting with unconfirmed claims about Democratic presidential nominee Joe Biden, leading President Trump's campaign and allies to charge the companies with censorship.
  Both social media companies said the moves were aimed at slowing the spread of potentially false information. But they gave few details about how they reached their decisions, sparking criticism about the lack of clarity and consistency with which they apply their rules.
     The  New York Post published a series of stories on Wednesday citing emails, purportedly sent by Biden's son Hunter, that the news outlet says it got from Trump's private lawyer, Rudy Giuliani, and former Trump adviser Steve Bannon.
  Facebook was limiting distribution of the  Post's main story while its outside fact-checkers reviewed the claims, spokesman Andy Stone said. That means the platform's algorithms won't place posts linking to the story as highly in people's news feeds, reducing the number of users who see it. However, the story has still been liked, shared or commented on almost 600,000 times on Facebook, according data from CrowdTangle, a research tool owned by the social network.
  Stone said Facebook sometimes takes this step if it sees " signals" that something gaining traction is false, to give fact-checkers time to evaluate the story before it spreads widely. He did not give more detail on what signals Facebook uses or how often it takes this approach.
  Twitter went further. It is blocking users from posting pictures of the emails or links to two of the  New York Post's stories referring to them, spokesman Trenton Kennedy said, citing its rules against sharing "content obtained through hacking that contains private information."
      Users who try to share the links on Twitter are shown a notice saying, "We can't complete this request because this link has been identified by Twitter or our partners as being potentially harmful."
  If a user clicks on links already posted on Twitter, the user is taken to a warning screen saying, "this link may be unsafe," which they have to click past to read the story. Twitter also required the  New York Post to delete its tweet about the story.
  Twitter said it decided to block the links because it couldn't be sure about the origins of the emails. It said its policy "prohibits the use of our service to distribute content obtained without authorization" and that it doesn't want to encourage hacking by allowing people to share "possibly illegally obtained materials."
  But the company declined to comment on how it had reached that decision or what evidence it had weighed about the emails in the  Post's stories.
    Its safety team  said in a tweet that the images of emails in the articles "include personal and private information — like email addresses and phone numbers — which violate our rules" against unauthorized sharing of such details.
  CEO Jack Dorsey  acknowledged that the company's communication about why it was blocking the articles "was not great." He tweeted that it was "unacceptable" to prevent people from sharing "with zero context as to why we're blocking."
  Asked for comment about the social networks' actions,  New York Post spokeswoman Iva Benson referred NPR  to an  article by the paper's editorial board.
  "Our story explains where the info came from, and a Senate committee now confirms it also received the files from the same source," the editorial said. "Yet Facebook and Twitter are deliberately trying to keep its users from reading and deciding for themselves what it means."
     Twitter and  Facebook have been acting more aggressively in recent weeks to curb the spread of false claims and manipulation related to the election, as part of efforts to avoid a repeat of 2016, when Russian-linked actors used social media to target American voters.
  Facebook has been  warning about the possibility of "hack and leak" operations, where stolen documents or other sensitive materials are strategically leaked — as happened in 2016 with hacked emails from the Democratic National Committee and Hillary Clinton's campaign.
  But the companies' moves on Wednesday drew criticism from some experts, who said Facebook and Twitter needed to more clearly explain their policies and how often they apply them.
  "This story is a microcosm of something that I think we can expect to happen a lot over the next few weeks and, I think, demonstrates why platforms having clear policies that they are prepared to stick to is really important," said Evelyn Douek, a Harvard Law School lecturer who studies the regulation of online speech.
  "It's really unclear if they have stepped in exceptionally in this case and, if they have, why they've done so," she said. "That inevitably leads to exactly the kind of outcry that we've seen, which is that they're doing it for political reasons and because they're biased."
  Republicans seized on the episode as proof of their  long-running claims that the social networks censor conservative voices. There is no statistical evidence to support those claims.
     Trump  tweeted that it was "So terrible that Facebook and Twitter took down the story," although Facebook did not remove it from its platform. "REPEAL SECTION 230!!!" he wrote, referring to a long-standing  legal shield that protects online platforms from being sued over what people post on them and says they can't be punished for reasonable moderation of those posts. Trump has repeatedly called for Section 230 to be  revoked.
  Sen. Josh Hawley of Missouri sent letters to the CEOs of both  Facebook and  Twitter on Wednesday pressing them on the decisions to reduce distribution and block the story.
  Hawley also sent a letter to the  Federal Election Commission saying the companies' actions possibly constituted "egregious campaign-finance violations benefitting the Biden campaign."
  The Senate Republican Conference, which is led by Sen. John Barrasso, R-Wyo.,  tweeted, "see you soon, @jack," with an image of the  Post's story. Twitter CEO Jack Dorsey is scheduled to testify, along with Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg and Google CEO Sundar Pichai, before the Senate Commerce Committee on Oct. 28 — just six days before the election. The topic: Section 230.
title: Facebook和Twitter限制分享《纽约邮报》关于乔·拜登的故事
title_en: Facebook and Twitter Limit Sharing New York Post Story About Joe Biden

如果您曾经使用SSH密钥管理过多台机器，那么您很有可能使用过SSH-agent。此工具旨在将SSH密钥保存在内存中，以便用户不必每次都键入他们的密码短语。但是，这可能会造成一些安全风险。以根用户身份运行的用户可能具有从内存中提取解密的SSH密钥并重新构建它的能力。
由于需要超级用户访问权限，此攻击可能看起来无用。例如，攻击者可以安装键盘记录程序并使用它来获取SSH密钥的密码短语。但是，这会导致攻击者不得不等待目标键入其密码。这可能是小时、天或周，具体取决于目标注销的频率。这就是从内存中获取SSH密钥对于快速转移到其他机器至关重要的原因。
使用SSH-agent的一种常见方法是运行“SSH-agent bash”，然后运行“SSH-add”将密钥添加到代理。一旦添加，密钥将保留在SSH代理的堆栈中，直到进程结束、添加另一个密钥或用户将-d或-D选项与SSH-add一起使用。大多数人会运行一次，然后忘记它，直到他们需要重新启动。
有几种方法可以创建SSH代理内存的副本。最简单的方法是使用gdb。Gdb使用ptrace调用附加到SSH代理。这为gdb提供了创建正在运行的进程的内存转储所需的权限。Sh脚本提供了自动转储此内存的方法。默认情况下，当它运行时，它将为每个SSH代理进程创建堆栈的内存转储。这些文件被命名为SSHagent-PID.stack。
如果gdb在系统上不可用，那么获取整个机器的内存转储并使用易失性来提取SSH代理进程的堆栈可能是可行的。但是，此过程目前不在本文档的讨论范围内。
一旦我们有了堆栈的副本，就可以从该文件中提取密钥。但是，密钥以与SSH-keygen生成的格式不同的格式保存在堆栈中。这就是parse_mem.py脚本派上用场的地方。此脚本需要安装pyasn1 python模块。一旦安装完毕，就可以针对内存文件运行脚本了。如果该内存文件包含有效的RSA SSH密钥，则会将其保存到磁盘。该工具的未来版本可能支持其他密钥格式，如DSA、ECDSA、ED25519和RSA1。
然后，此key.rsa文件可以用作SSH中-i开关的参数。这将与原始用户的密钥相似，只是不需要密码短语即可解锁。
获得有效的、可用的SSH密钥可以帮助渗透测试员获得对客户端网络的进一步访问。在用户的帐户上以及服务器上的根帐户上使用密钥是很常见的。也可以将服务器配置为仅允许密钥访问。访问未加密的密钥可以使在环境中移动变得容易得多。
我不得不说，这似乎很明显。这就是为什么keychain和ssh-agent首先有超时选项的原因，不是吗？是一种风险和便利性的平衡。Https://unix.stackexchange.com/questions/32609/can-i-set-how-long-ssh-agent-keeps-keys-in-memory http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1271580。
超时的好处是你可以把自己设定在你想要平衡的地方。
我认为，当用户处于root状态并尝试执行以下操作时，这是不太可能的情况。考虑到用户在根目录中具有访问权限，另一种方法是扫描HDD中的.rsa文件并恢复它们。再说一次，这是一篇很棒的文章，但在真实的工作环境中不太可能看到这种情况，除非管理员粗心大意的🙂。
旧新闻…。就像我的一位水手说的那样，2012年来了电话，并在语音信箱中留下了这些信息：
note_en:
If you've ever used SSH keys to manage multiple machines, then chances are you've used SSH-agent. This tool is designed to keep a SSH key in memory so that the user doesn't have to type their passphrase in every time. However, this can create some security risk. A user running as root may have the ability to pull the decrypted SSH key from memory and reconstruct it.
 Due to needing root access, this attack may seem useless. For example, an attacker may be able to install a keylogger and use that to obtain the passphrase for the SSH key. However, this causes the attacker to have to wait for the target to type in their passphrase. This might be hours, days, or weeks, depending on how often the target logs out. This is why obtaining the SSH key from memory is vital to pivoting to other machines in a speedy fashion.
  A common method of using SSH-agent is running “SSH-agent bash” and then “SSH-add” to add the key to the agent. Once added, the key will stay in the SSH-agent's stack until the process ends, another key is added, or the user uses the -d or -D option with SSH-add. Most people will run this once and then forget about it until they need to reboot.
  There are a few ways to create a copy of the SSH-agents memory. The easiest way is through the use of gdb. Gdb uses the ptrace call to attach to the SSH-agent. This provides gdb with the privileges necessary to create a memory dump of the running process. The  grabagentmem.sh script provides a way of automating the dumping of this memory. By default, when it runs it will create a memory dump of the stack for each SSH-agent process. These files are named SSHagent-PID.stack.
  If gdb is not available on the system, then it might be feasible to take a memory dump of the entire machine and use volatility to extract the stack of the SSH-agent processes. However, this process is currently out of the scope for this document.
  Once we have a copy of the stack it becomes possible to extract the key from this file. However, the key is kept in the stack in a different format then the one that was generated by SSH-keygen. This is where the  parse_mem.py script comes in handy. This script requires the installation of the pyasn1 python module. Once that is installed the script can be run against the memory file. If that memory file contains a valid RSA SSH key then it will save it to disk. Future versions of the tool may support additional key formats, such as DSA, ECDSA, ED25519, and RSA1.
  This key.rsa file can then be used as an argument to the -i switch in SSH. This will act like the original user's key, only without requiring a pass phrase to unlock it.
 Obtaining valid, usable SSH keys can help a penetration tester gain further access into a client's network. It's common for keys to be used on both the user's account, as well as the root account on servers. It is also possible that a server is configured to only allow key access. Having access to an unencrypted key can make moving around the environment much easier.
     I have to say, this seems kinda obvious. This is why keychain and ssh-agent have timeout options in the first place, no? Is kind of a balance of risk versus convenience.  https://unix.stackexchange.com/questions/32609/can-i-set-how-long-ssh-agent-keeps-keys-in-memory  http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1271580
 The nice thing about timeout is you can set yourself where you want to set that balance.
 Well I think this is an unlikely case when the user is in root and tries to do something like this. Another way considering that the user has access in root is to scan the hdd for .rsa files and recover them. Again, a great article but it’s not likely to see this happen in a real working environment unless the admin is careless 🙂
 Old news… Like an aquaitenace of mine said, 2012 has called and left these messages in the voicemail:
title: 从内存中窃取未加密的SSH代理密钥(2014)
title_en: Stealing unencrypted SSH-agent keys from memory (2014)

应用程序操作农场听起来像是开发人员周末休息的地方，还有脊椎按摩师课程。事实上-根据一张在中国社交媒体上疯传的照片-这是一个开发者可以为他们的应用程序的下载量被人为夸大买单的地方。
为什么会有人想要这么做呢？简单地说：因为更多的下载量(可能伴随着正面评论)提高了应用程序在排行榜上的位置，从而提高了它们的可发现性水平，并有望促使人们下载它们。
这张有问题的照片似乎显示了一名工作人员坐在一个这样的地方，坐在看起来像是大约100部iPhone5c的东西前面。报道称，她的工作是反复下载、安装和卸载特定的应用程序，以提高他们的App Store排名。在她对面可以看到另一张类似的桌子。
这张图片还附有第二张图片，显示了为了让你的应用程序登上App Store排行榜榜首而收取的所谓价格。以下是你需要支付多少钱才能为自己获得一款排名第一的应用程序：
据称，这份价格表称，一名希望进入前十名免费应用程序的人将花费11,200美元。要想留在那里，他们每周需要支付6.5万美元。
通过使用中国最受欢迎的C2C电子商务网站淘宝搜索“应用商店排名操纵”，可以找到无数类似的服务。尽管苹果对操纵App Store排名不屑一顾，但对于当地企业家来说，这样的计划是一棵经过认证的摇钱树--即使你需要花钱购买大量的手机和员工。
note_en:
An app manipulation farm sounds like someplace developers would go for a weekend retreat, complete with chiropractor sessions. In fact — according to a photo which has gone viral on social media in China — it’s a place where devs can pay for their apps’ download numbers to be artificially inflated.
 Why would anyone want to do this? Simply put: because more downloads (perhaps accompanied by positive reviews) enhances apps’ chart position, thereby raising their discoverability level, and hopefully prompting people to download them.
 The photo in question appears to show a worker at one such place, sitting in front of what look like around 100 iPhone 5c units. Reports claim that her job is download, install, and uninstall specific apps repeatedly to boost their App Store rankings. Another similar table can be seen opposite her.
 The image is accompanied by a second one, showing the alleged prices being charged to get your app to the top of the App Store rankings. Here’s how much you need to pay to secure a no. 1 rated app for yourself:
     The alleged price sheet claims that a person looking to get into the top 10 free apps list is looking at spending $11,200. To stay there, they will then need to shell out $65,000 per week.
  By searching for “app store ranking manipulation” using China’s most popular C2C e-commerce site, Taobao, numerous similar services can be found. As much as Apple frowns on manipulation of the App Store rankings, for local entrepreneurs a scheme like this is a certified cash cow — even with the large number of handsets and employees you need to spend money on.
title: 疯狂的iPhone平台展示中国工人如何操纵App Store排名
title_en: Crazy iPhone rig shows how Chinese workers manipulate App Store rankings

note_en: Going off-grid? Think twice before you invest in a battery system. Compressed air energy storage is the sustainable and resilient alternative to batteries, with much longer life expectancy, lower life cycle costs, technical simplicity, and low maintenance. Designing a compressed air energy storage system that combines high efficiency with small storage size is not self-explanatory, but a growing number of researchers show that it can be done. This article has been translated into French, Spanish and Dutch. Compressed Air Energy Storage (CAES) is usually regarded as a form of large-scale energy storage, comparable to a pumped hydropower plant. Such a CAES plant compresses air and stores it in an underground cavern, recovering the energy by expanding (or decompressing) the air...

袁在80年代从中国移民到美国。他听过比尔·盖茨关于互联网的演讲，他想成为数字革命的一部分。
9次尝试。埃里克·袁花了9次努力才最终拿到了去美国的签证。
他在那里工作了十年，后来晋升为工程副总裁。
在袁的领导下，WebEx发展到750多名工程师，年收入约10亿美元(后来被思科收购)。可以肯定地说，该公司经营得很好。
袁会和客户见面，他们会不高兴的。他们会抱怨视频和音频滞后。连接问题。各种各样的东西。
在我离开思科之前，我花了很多时间与WebEx客户交谈，每次与他们交谈时我都感到非常尴尬，因为我没有看到一个满意的客户，我试图理解为什么会这样。袁耀威。
因此，在2011年，袁离开了。他决定创办自己的公司，使命是打造世界上最好的视频会议软件。
他离开后，与他共事的800名工程师中有40人立即加入了他的Zoom。
根据这条推文，几乎所有其他人都寄来了与他共事的简历。在宣布离职后的一周内，他收到了大约1000份求职咨询。
今天，Zoom的估值超过400亿美元。大流行可能加速了公司的增长，但不要搞错了：这是9年来一夜之间取得的成功。
我认为最让我印象深刻的是袁亚非确实有勇气离开思科，继续创办Zoom。
经常有很大的惰性阻止我们离开我们不满意的工作。
以袁为例，从他移民到美国开始，他的职业生涯无论从哪方面来看都是非常成功的。大多数处在他的位置上的人甚至不会辞去工程副总裁这个舒适的职位。
因此，他本可以安顿下来，听天由命地接受这样一个事实：也许视频会议软件应该是这样的。毕竟，市场上的竞争非常少。
但他没有。他选择了不舒服，成百上千的其他工程师相信他。
继续前进，不要停滞不前。你可能会惊讶于有这么多人关注你。
有些人称它为时事通讯，我称它为美好时光。我写的是技术职业，以及你如何在你的职业生涯中取得成功。这是我最好的内容(就像这个案例研究)，每周向您提供一次。
note_en:
Yuan migrated to the USA from China in the 80s. He had heard Bill Gates speak about the internet and he wanted to be a part of the digital revolution.
  9 tries. It took Eric Yuan 9 tries to finally get his visa for the USA.
  He worked there for a decade and rose up the ranks to become the VP of Engineering.
 Under Yuan’s leadership, WebEx grew to more than 750 engineers and had an annual revenue of around $1B (and was later acquired by Cisco). Safe to say the company was doing well.
   Yuan would meet with customers and they would be unhappy. They’d complain about video and audio lag. Connectivity issues. All sorts of stuff.
  Before I left Cisco I spent a lot of time talking to WebEx customers and every time I talked to them I felt very embarrassed because I did not see a single happy customer, and I tried to understand why that was.  Eric Yuan
 So in 2011, Yuan left. He decided to start his own company with the mission of building the best video conferencing software in the world.
 After he left, 40 of the 800 engineers he worked with immediately joined him at Zoom.
 And according to  this tweet, almost all of the others sent in resumes to work with him. He had something like 1000 job inquiries within a week of announcing his leaving.
  Today Zoom is valued at more than $40 billion. The pandemic may have accelerated the company’s growth but make no mistake: this was an overnight success 9 years in the making.
  I think the part that stands out the most to me is that Yuan actually had the courage to leave Cisco and go on to start Zoom.
 There is often great inertia that prevents us from leaving jobs we’re unhappy with.
 In Yuan’s case, he had by all measures a very successful career ever since he immigrated to the US. Most people in his shoes wouldn’t even bother resigning from a comfortable VP of Engineering position.
 So he could have settled and just resigned himself to the fact that maybe video conferencing software was supposed to be like this. After all, there was very little competition in the market.
 But he didn’t. He chose discomfort and hundreds of other engineers believed in him.
 Keep moving forward and don’t settle. You might be surprised at how many people follow you.
        Some people call it a newsletter - I call it a good time. I write about tech careers and how you can get ahead in yours. It’s my best content (like this case study) delivered to you once a week.
title: 埃里克·袁的签证申请被拒绝了8次
title_en: Eric Yuan's Visa Application Was Rejected 8 Times

2018年，当一家独立游戏公司在我们中间成立时，它受到了低调的欢迎。和许多游戏一样，多人游戏一直不为人所知-直到大流行的夏天。
为了在隔离期间让观众保持娱乐，Chance Morris，在网上被称为Sodapoppin，从7月份开始向他在Twitch上的280万粉丝流媒体这款由InnerSloth开发的游戏。
到九月中旬，我们中间就像野火一样流行起来。突然间，YouTube的主要明星、TikTok的影响者和流媒体用户都在播放它。PewDiePie，James Charles和Dr.Lupo都玩过数百万美元的游戏。
转发成为下一个视频的妹妹大喊答题！！第一次在我们中间比赛！以一些非常特别的客人为特色，https://t.co/NhpxZf4mdR😇🔪pic.twitter.com/kkOCQJf3Qk。
-詹姆斯·查尔斯(@Jamescharles)2020年10月6日。
我们之间是一个多人游戏，4到10名玩家被投到一艘外星宇宙飞船上。每个玩家都被指定一个私人角色，作为“船员”或“冒名顶替者”。
船员必须在船上跑来跑去，试图完成一系列任务，同时试图铲除并避免被一个或几个冒名顶替者杀死。玩家可以投票下船，所以每一场比赛都成为一场生存的游戏：成功地投票击败冒名顶替者，或者完成所有的任务才能获胜。它简单、卡通，对于一个5岁的孩子来说，在iPhone上玩就足够容易了。
网络影响力的年轻粉丝不仅在我们中间观看流媒体，他们也开始玩这个游戏。全国数以百万计的青少年和儿童已经迷上了我们，这已经开始成为被隔离的年轻人的默认社交平台。
德克萨斯州16岁的高中生朱达·赖斯(Judah Rice)说：“几周前，我从什么都没听说到，现在到处都能听到有关它的一切。”“人们都在发短信谈论这件事，我认识一些人，他们使用专用的不和谐服务器，在我们中间进行群聊。我有朋友经常聚在一起玩这个游戏。“。
在美国，不和谐服务器从9月初开始出现。在其中一个活动中，超过9.8万名青少年连接、社交、讨论和玩这个游戏。本森是该服务器的一名13岁的管理员，他说，在一天中的任何时候，我们之间经常有30到40个不同的游戏通过服务器上的语音聊天频道发生。“每个人的年龄都在13岁到20岁之间，”他说。“我的朋友们，如果他们的老师没有出现在他们的在线课堂上，他们就玩，这是一种在你无事可做的时候打发时间的方式。因为我们不能真的聚集在公园这样的公共区域，所以我们中间的人让我们可以在线社交距离。“。
例如，“我们中间的人”与其他高度社交化的电子游戏，比如“堡垒之夜”，有很大的不同。它更类似于像大富翁这样的棋盘游戏，或者像狼人这样的派对游戏，在这些游戏中，玩家需要阅读人物性格，并确定自己是否被欺骗才能获胜。庞大的群规模使得邀请新朋友加入群变得很容易。
17岁的胡安·阿隆索·弗洛雷斯(Juan Alonso Flores)是佛罗里达州的一名高中生，他说：“你可以在我们中间认识很多人，我用它交了几个朋友。”“一旦你开始和同样的人一起玩，你就会开始了解对方。你会得到他们的电话号码，不和谐标签。“。
不仅仅是青少年在游戏中建立了联系。那些下班后不能再去酒吧或聚会上摇摆的成年人也在我们中间找到了社区。36岁的里奇·海伯格(Ricky Hayberg)是YouTube文化和科技频道“今日互联网”(Internet Today)的撰稿人和主持人，他说，过去两个月里，他与通过“我们之间的游戏”认识的人建立了更牢固的友谊，而不是多年来他在线下认识的人。
“由此产生了更自然的对话。这更像是一场派对游戏。你只是和朋友一起闲逛，游戏是次要的，“海伯格说。“要想在这项运动中脱颖而出，你必须知道人们是否在撒谎，是否在说真话，以及他们的一般个性。”
互联网上源源不断的与美国相关的内容进一步推动了这款游戏的持续成功。推特账号如“我们之间没有上下文”和“我们之间的斗争”推特向数十万粉丝发布了关于这个游戏的表情包。TikTok上的视频，包括我们之间的标签，已经积累了超过130亿的点击量。
17岁的妮可·德雷珀(Nicole Draper)在她的Instagram表情包页面Bruhloon上发布了我们中的表情包，她说，“炒作持续增长，因为每个人都在玩它。”“我的TikTok For You页面上充斥着我们中间的表情包。人们对“我们之间”的场景进行恶搞和画外音。它变得比其他任何游戏都要大。“
在这款游戏的整个崛起过程中，Twitch上的流媒体继续为它提供动力。Twitch创作者营销社区主任艾琳·韦恩(Erin Wayne)说：“如果有人怀疑Twitch流媒体在此之前是有影响力的，我们已经证明了这一点是真的。”
今年到目前为止，Twitch的粉丝们已经在这个平台上观看了价值超过2亿小时的《我们之间》游戏。游戏网站Kotaku的撰稿人内森·格雷森(Nathan Grayson)表示，这款游戏的设计独特，使其观看起来比普通的第一人称射击游戏更具娱乐性。
观众可以看到有影响力的人放下防备，表达他们的真实面貌。格雷森说，“这个游戏关乎欺骗和玩家的个性。”“它带回了和朋友在一起的精神。它的设计目的是让流媒体用户相互竞争，他们也可以拥有流媒体，所有的流媒体都在不同的频道上流媒体，观众在聊天中猜测哪个人是冒名顶替者。“。
28岁的喜剧演员菲尔·詹姆森(Phil Jamesson)在Twitch上进行直播，他说，这款游戏类似于“辣妹”这样的系列剧，粉丝们可以看到他们最喜欢的名人放下门面，努力忍受越来越辣的鸡翅。
“当情绪高涨时，障碍就会降低。当有人指责你在游戏中杀害朋友时，你可以窥探他们的真实面目，而不是他们想要展示自己的样子。“他说。
人们已经在利用围绕这场比赛的兴奋来达到政治目的。MoveOn是一个进步的倡导组织和政治行动委员会，它一直在美国Twitch上与正义民主党人和弯曲媒体等组织以及知名活动人士合作，鼓励年轻观众投票。
“虚拟组织现在真的很流行，”19岁的学生兼活动家卡梅隆·卡斯基(Cameron Kasky)说。“我们中间是一个很棒的地方，可以谈论任何你想在那里得到的东西。”你得到了人们的关注和关注，这个游戏并不太复杂，谈论投票会让人分心。它可以让观众在视觉上全神贯注，而你可以随心所欲地谈论。“
note_en:
When an indie game company created  Among Us in 2018, it was greeted with little fanfare. The multiplayer game remained under the radar as many games do — until the summer of the pandemic.
 Eager to keep viewers entertained during quarantine, Chance Morris, known online as Sodapoppin, began  streaming the game, created by InnerSloth, to his 2.8 million followers on Twitch in July.
 By mid-September, Among Us caught on like wildfire. Suddenly major YouTube stars, TikTok influencers and streamers were playing it. PewDiePie, James Charles and Dr. Lupo have all played the game for millions.
 RETWEET to be the next video's sister shoutout!!  PLAYING AMONG US FOR THE FIRST TIME! featuring some very special guests 😇🔪  https://t.co/NhpxZf4mdR  pic.twitter.com/KkOCQJf3Qk
— James Charles (@jamescharles)  October 6, 2020
 Among Us is a multiplayer game where between four and 10 players are dropped onto an alien spaceship. Each player is designated a private role as a “crewmate” or “impostor.”
 Crewmates must run around the ship and try to complete a set of tasks while trying to root out and avoid getting killed by the one or several impostors. Players can be voted off the ship, so each game becomes one of survival: Successfully vote off the impostors, or complete all your tasks to win. It’s simple, cartoony and easy enough for a five-year-old to play on an iPhone.
 The young fans of online influencers weren’t just watching Among Us streams, they began playing the game too. Millions of teenagers and kids across the country have become hooked on Among Us, which has begun to serve as a default social platform for young people stuck in quarantine.
 “A few weeks ago I went from not hearing anything about it to hearing everything about it everywhere,” said Judah Rice, 16, a high school student in Texas. “People are texting about it, I know people who are on dedicated Discord servers and Among Us group chats. I have friends who get together all the time and play it.”
 Among Us Discord servers began cropping up in early September. In one, more than 98,000 teenagers connect, socialize, discuss and play the game. Benson, a 13-year-old administrator of the server, said that there are often 30 to 40 different Among Us games at any time of day happening over voice chat channels in the server. “Everyone is aged 13 to 20,” he said. “My friends, if their teacher doesn’t show up to their online class, they play, it’s a way to pass the time when you don’t have anything else to do. Since we can’t really congregate in a public area like the park, Among Us allows us to be online social distancing.”
 Among Us is very different than other highly social video games like Fortnite, for instance. It’s more similar to a board game like Monopoly, or a party game like Werewolf, where players need to read personalities and determine if they’re being lied to in order to win. The large group size makes it easy to invite new friends into the group.
 “You can meet a lot of people from Among Us, I’ve made a few friends off of it,” said Juan Alonso Flores, 17, a high school student in Florida. “Once you start playing with the same people you start getting to know each other. You get their phone numbers, Discord tags.”
  It’s not just teenagers who are bonding over the game. Adults who can no longer hit a bar or swing by a party after work are also finding community through Among Us. Ricky Hayberg, 36, writer and host of  Internet Today, a culture and tech YouTube channel, said he’s developed stronger friendships with people he met through playing Among Us over the past two months than those he’s known offline for years.
 “There’s more natural conversation that arises from it. It’s more of a party game. You’re kind of just hanging out with friends and the game is secondary,” Mr. Hayberg said. “To excel at the game, you have to know if people are lying, telling the truth and their general personality.”
 The game’s continued success is further propelled by a never-ending stream of Among Us-related content on the internet. Twitter accounts like  No Context Among Us and  Among Us Struggle Tweets publish memes about the game to hundreds of thousands of followers. Videos on TikTok including the Among Us hashtag have amassed more than 13 billion views.
 “The hype keeps growing because everyone is playing it,” said Nicole Draper, 17, who posts Among Us memes on  Bruhloon, her Instagram meme page. “My TikTok For You page is flooded with Among Us memes. People are making parodies and voice-overs of Among Us scenes. It grew bigger than any other game.”
  Throughout the game’s ascendance, streamers on Twitch continue to power it all. “If anyone had any doubt that Twitch streamers were influencers before now, we’ve proven that to be true,” said Erin Wayne, director of community of creator marketing at Twitch.
 Twitch fans have watched more than 200 million hours worth of Among Us gameplay on the platform so far this year. Nathan Grayson, a writer at gaming site Kotaku, said that the game was uniquely designed in a way that makes it far more entertaining to watch than your average first person shooter game.
 Viewers get to see influencers let their guards down and express how they really are. “The game is about deception and the personalities of people playing,” Mr. Grayson said. “It brings back the spirit of being around your friends. It’s designed for streamers to play off one another and they can also have streams where all of them are streaming on separate channels with their audiences speculating on chat about which person is an impostor.”
 Phil Jamesson, 28, a comedian who streams on Twitch, said that the game is similar to a series like “ Hot Ones,” where fans get to see their favorite celebrities drop the facade as they struggle to tolerate increasingly spicy chicken wings.
  “There’s a barrier that gets lowered when there’s heightened emotions. When someone is accusing you of killing a friend in the game, you get a peek into how they actually are, as opposed to how they want to present themselves,” he said.
 Already, people are leveraging excitement around the game for political ends. MoveOn, a progressive advocacy organization and political action committee has been streaming Among Us games on Twitch with organizations like Justice Democrats and Crooked Media, and well-known activists to encourage young viewers to vote.
 “Virtual organizing is really popping off right now,” said Cameron Kasky, 19, a student and activist. “Among Us is a great place to talk about really anything you want to get out there. You’ve got people’s eyes and attention and the game is not too complicated where it’d be distracting to talk about voting. It leaves the viewers visually engaged while you talk about whatever you want.”
title: 无处可去，青少年成群结队地涌入我们中间
title_en: With Nowhere to Go, Teens Flock to Among Us

到2021年，佛罗里达州居民将可以申请新的移动驾照，这些驾照可以在包括智能手机和平板电脑在内的各种设备上轻松获得。
这项服务将由泰利斯公司提供，该公司设计和建造电气系统，并为航空航天、国防、运输和安全市场提供服务。
泰利斯的一份声明称，佛罗里达州将成为美国第一个提供具有尖端安全机制的移动驾照的州，完全符合严格的国家和国际标准。
其他几个州已经测试了数字驾照，路易斯安那州已经为其数字驾照洛杉矶钱包开了绿灯，这是由一家名为Envoc的公司开发的。
FLDHSMV的执行董事特里·L·罗兹(Terry L.Rhodes)说，“我对这个创新项目感到兴奋，它将使佛罗里达州在提供安全可信的移动身份识别方面成为全国领先的州，这是我们州长罗恩·德桑蒂斯(Ron DeSantis)的优先事项。”我们对我们部门工作的几乎每一个方面以及客户如何获得我们的服务的现代化做出了持续的承诺，随着佛罗里达州现在步入移动识别的未来，泰利斯公司将成为一个很好的合作伙伴。“。
根据泰利斯的说法，数字许可证的工作方式将与传统许可证相同。人们会打开这款应用程序，展示它来验证你的年龄，在TSA办理登机手续，或者与执法部门互动。然而，到目前为止，泰利斯公司在他们的网站上声明，这将取决于每个州和地方执法机构来决定哪些程序和方法在他们现有的协议中工作得最好。
note_en:
In 2021, Florida residents will be able to apply for new mobile driver's licenses that are easily accessible on a variety of devices, including smartphones and tablets.
   The service will be provided by the company Thales, which designs and builds electrical systems and provides services for the aerospace, defense, transportation and security markets.
 "The State of Florida will be the first state in the United States to provide mobile Driver Licenses with leading-edge security mechanisms, fully compliant with rigorous national and international standards.," a statement from Thales said.
 Several other states have tested digital licenses and Louisiana has already given the green light to its digital driver's license, the LA Wallet, developed by a company called Envoc.
 “I’m excited for this innovative project that will make the state of Florida a national leader in offering secure and trusted mobile identification, which is a priority for our governor, Ron DeSantis,” said Terry L. Rhodes, executive director of the FLDHSMV. “We have made a sustained commitment to the modernization of nearly every aspect of what our department does and how customers access our services, and Thales will be a great partner as Florida now steps into the future of mobile identification.”
 According to Thales, a digital license will work the same way as a traditional one. People would open the app and present it to verify your age, check in at TSA or interact with law enforcement. As of now, though, Thales states on their website, "It will be up to each state and local law enforcement agency to determine what procedure and methods work best within their existing protocol."
title: 佛罗里达州可能成为第一个提供数字驾照的州

note:
德国博学者和耶稣会牧师阿萨纳修斯·基尔彻一生痴迷于声音，并专门出版了两本书：Musurgia Universalis(1650年)，关于理论(和神学)方面，和Phonurgia Nova(1673年)，关于声学的科学及其实际应用。因此，得知他位于罗马学院的著名博物馆除了拥有“呕吐的雕像”、能召唤鬼魂的镜子和其他奇妙的东西外，也就不足为奇了--收藏了种类繁多的乐器。
我们今天对基尔彻博物馆的了解在很大程度上要归功于他的学生和耶稣会牧师菲利波·布纳尼(Filippo Buonanni，1638-1725年)，他接替基尔彻担任数学教授，并在基尔彻去世后成为该博物馆的首席保管人，1709年，他为该博物馆制作了一本史诗般的、详尽的、近800页的目录。追随大师的脚步，布纳尼也有着令人眼花缭乱的兴趣和专长，包括钱币学、显微镜、自然生成、中国漆器、贝类(他在这些贝类上创作了第一本专著)，还有，就像基尔彻一样，他也喜欢音乐。
受Kircher的神童乐器收藏的启发，并对其背后的故事感兴趣，1722年，Buonanni出版了他的Gabinetto Armonico Pieno d‘istromenti Sonori(或充满声波实例的和声橱柜)，试图首次将世界乐器编录在目录中。在1722年，Buonanni出版了他的Gabinetto Armonico Pieno d’istromenti Sonori(或充满声波实例的和声橱柜)，试图首次对世界乐器进行分类。虽然每种乐器都有简短而富有启发性的文字，但真正抢占风头的是由佛兰德艺术家兼出版商阿诺德·范·韦斯特胡特(Arnold Van Westhout)制作的152个雕刻版面。特色乐器分为三个部分-管乐、弦乐和打击乐-在此之前，还对其他音乐类别进行了13个简短的讨论，包括：军事、葬礼、祭祀中使用的，以及有趣的是，在海上使用的：不是警笛，而是吟唱的水手。虽然布诺纳尼的书中收集的一些乐器很简单，比如养蜂人敲打浴缸，或者鞋子在地板上咔哒作响，但也有一些是精心制作的技术性机器；维罗斯比宫殿(Palazzo Verospi)的伟大管风琴需要一页折叠的页面才能展示所有这些东西。在韦罗斯皮宫(Palazzo Verospi)，这台伟大的管风琴需要一页折叠的页面才能展示所有的东西。我们还会听到一些可能被认为更附带的乐器，例如，关于被捆绑的罪犯脖子的铃声，以及士兵剑被击中的声音。
背景中的一些乐器看起来很奇特，你会注意到音乐家中的潘和小天使。他们很可能看起来很奇特，因为，正如Deirdre Loughbridge和Thomas Patteson在他们的文章“猫钢琴、音响房子和其他想象中的乐器”中解释的那样，像“Tubo Cochleato”这样的乐器还不存在；Gabinetto列出了实际存在的和预计的乐器；那些已经或者应该有一天被建造的乐器。
这本书像百科全书一样具有人类学意义，它不仅展示了乐器，还展示了他们的音乐家-甚至是女性-因此，它符合旅行写作和外国人民目录的流行传统。除了博纳尼欧洲的民间和古老传统，加比内托还包括来自非洲、亚洲和美洲的乐器和音乐家，经常伴随着民族志遭遇的细节。约翰·怀特(John White)和西奥多·德布里(Theodore De Bry)等16世纪探险家的读者可能会发现，“佛罗里达特罗姆巴”(Tromba della佛罗里达)对这位音乐家的服装、发型和风景的描绘很熟悉。Buonanni的Gabinetto从音乐、民族和世界中获得了意义和清晰，可以认为它本身就是一种和谐理解的工具。
你可以浏览我们下面的版画亮点，也可以在这里完整地扫描这本书的上下文中看到它们。

note_en:
The German polymath and Jesuit priest  Athanasius Kircher had a lifelong fascination with sound and devoted two books to the subject:   Musurgia Universalis (1650), on the theoretical (and theological) aspects, and   Phonurgia Nova (1673), on the science of acoustics and its practical applications. It's no surprise then to learn that his famed museum at Rome's Collegio Romano boasted— in addition to  “vomiting statues”, ghost-conjuring mirrors, and other curious wonders — a vast and diverse collection of musical instruments.
 Much of what we know of Kircher's museum today is thanks to his student and fellow Jesuit priest Filippo Buonanni (1638-1725), who succeeded Kircher as both Professor of Mathematics and, upon Kircher's death, as chief custodian of the museum for which he produced an  epic and exhaustive, near-800-page catalogue in 1709. Following in his master's footsteps, Buonanni too held a dizzying array of interests and specialisms including numismatics, microscopy, spontaneous generation, Chinese laquer, seashells (on which he produced the first monograph), and also, like Kircher, music.
 Inspired by the collection of instruments in Kircher's  wunderkammer, and intrigued by the stories behind them, in 1722 Buonanni published his  Gabinetto Armonico pieno d’istromenti sonori (or Harmonic cabinet full of sonic instances), an attempt to catalogue, for the first time, the musical instruments of the world. While there's a short and often illuminating text for each instrument it is the 152 engraved plates — executed by Flemish artist and publisher Arnold van Westerhout — which really steal the show. The featured instruments are divided into three sections — wind, string, and percussion — and preceded by thirteen brief discussions of other musical categories, including: military, funeral, used in sacrifices, and, intriguingly, as used at sea: not sirens, but chantying sailors. While some of the instruments gathered in Buonanni's book are as simple as the bee-keeper banging his tub, or the clacking of shoes against the floor, some are highly crafted, technical machines; the great organ at Palazzo Verospi requires a fold-out page to show it all. We are also treated to what might be considered more  incidental instruments, for example, the bell about a bound criminal's neck and the sound of a soldier's sword being struck.
 Some of the instruments in their settings seem outlandish; you’ll notice Pan and cherubs among the musicians. Well might they look fantastical, for, as Deirdre Loughridge and Thomas Patteson explain in their essay ” Cat Pianos, Sound-Houses, and Other Imaginary Musical Instruments”, such instruments as the “Tubo Cochleato” did not yet exist; the  Gabinetto lists both actually existing and projected instruments; ones that had been, or should one day be, constructed.
 As much anthropological as it is encyclopaedic, the book shows not just instruments, but their musicians, too — even women —and as such it fits into the popular tradition of travel writing and catalogues of foreign peoples. In addition to the folk and antiquity traditions of Buonanni's Europe, the  Gabinetto covers instruments and musicians from Africa, Asia, and the Americas, often accompanied by details of ethnographic encounters. Readers of sixteenth-century explorers such as  John White and Theodore de Bry might find the “Tromba della Florida” familiar, with its depiction of the clothing, hairstyle, and landscape of the musician. Making sense and clarity out of music, peoples, and the world, Buonanni’s  Gabinetto can be seen as itself an instrument of harmonious understanding.

这笔钱来自洛杉矶娱乐界和投资界的重量级人物，包括斯库特·布劳恩、特洛伊·卡特、凯戈的棕榈树剧组和贾里德·莱托。Patreon首席执行官杰克·孔特(Jack Conte)和红杉资本(Sequoia Capital)合伙人杰斯·李(Jess Lee)也参加了融资。
先行者风险投资公司是这笔交易的领头羊，这笔投资是由该公司著名的创始人和管理合伙人柯尔斯滕·格林(Kirsten Green)进行的。TikTok前首席执行官凯文·梅耶尔(Kevin Mayer)、GV首席执行官大卫·克莱恩(David Krane)、Box的亚伦·列维(Aaron Levie)、科技媒体和娱乐大师马修·鲍尔(Matthew Ball)以及产品大师尤金·魏(Eugene魏)也参与了这轮融资。
这家公司由阿尔琼·梅塔(Arjun Mehta)、什雷·班萨尔(Shray Bansal)和奈杰尔·埃格拉里(Nigel Egrari)创立，由这三人在南加州大学吉米·约文博士创新学院(Jimmy Iovine&amp；Dr.Dre Academy for the Arts，Technology and the Business of Innovation)学习期间创立。
该公司吹捧自己是艺术家为粉丝创建在线活动的最简单方式。
作为第一次进军现场娱乐，时刻之家将为音乐家永布卢德举办一次有地理围栏的特定地点之旅。该公司表示，Kygo、黑熊、Kaytranada、丹泽尔·库里(Denzel Curry)和鲁尔(Ruel)的其他门票活动将紧随其后。
对于音乐家来说，该公司关于票务安全、商品整合和全球支付支持的宣传肯定对他们来说是一件乐事-因为所有这些功能加在一起就是一件事…。现金。
平台上的表演者拿走了所有的门票收入，时刻之家通过向粉丝收取少量费用来赚钱。
该公司联合创始人阿尔琼·梅塔(Arjun Mehta)在一份声明中表示，该公司的技术和服务并不是对新冠肺炎疫情的回应，而是一种通过在线近似放大演唱会体验的方式。
莱托在一份声明中说：“Moment House正在赋予艺术家们权力，让他们提供让人感觉真实和引人注目的数字体验。”“我被独特的设计驱动的方法所吸引，因为这是在这里创建一个新类别所需要的。”
note_en:
The money came from heavy hitters in the Los Angeles entertainment and investment scene including  Scooter Braun,    Troy Carter,   Kygo’s Palm Tree Crew and Jared Leto. Patreon chief executive Jack Conte and Sequoia Capital partner Jess Lee also participated in the round.
 Forerunner Ventures led the deal and the investment was made by Kirsten Green, the firm’s famous founder and managing partner. Kevin Mayer, the former chief executive of TikTok; GV chief David Krane; Aaron Levie from Box; the tech media and entertainment guru, Matthew Ball; and product maestro Eugene Wei all participated in the round as well.
   Founded by Arjun Mehta, Shray Bansal, and Nigel Egrari, the company grew out of work the three men did while attending the  USC Jimmy Iovine & Dr. Dre Academy for the Arts, Technology and the Business of Innovation.
 The company touts itself as the simplest way for artists to create online events for their fans.
 For its first foray into live entertainment, Moment House is going to host a geo-fenced, location-specific tour for the musician Yungblud. Other ticketed events from Kygo, blackbear, Kaytranada, Denzel Curry and Ruel will follow, the company said.
 For musicians, the company’s pitch of ticketing security, merchandise integrations global payments support, must have been music to their ears — because all of those features add up to one thing… cash.
 And performers on the platform take all of the ticket revenues, with Moment House earning money by charging fans a small fee.
 In a statement, company co-founder Arjun Mehta said that the company’s technology and service wasn’t a response to the COVID-19 pandemic, but rather a way to amplify the concert going experience with an online approximation.
 “Moment House is empowering artists to deliver digital experiences that feel authentic and compelling,” said Leto, in a statement . “I was drawn to the unique design-driven approach because that’s what is needed to create a new category here.”

note:
今年，我中学办公室的年终论文清洗揭示了我的教学实践中一个令人震惊的模式：我管教男生的次数远远多于管教女生的次数。翻阅粉色和黄色的纸条-我的学校向学生、导师和家长传达错误行为的系统-我发现，我向男孩发出的警告几乎是向女孩发出的警告的两倍，在我发给男孩的纸条中，除了一张之外，所有的都是关于破坏性课堂行为的警告。
今年我经历过的最令人沮丧的时刻源于这些争夺--也是为了--我的男学生们注意力的争斗。今年春天，随着足球场上的草变绿了，新汉普郡的空气终于升到了冰点以上，我和男孩们就他们在拉丁语和英语课上的智力和情感投入进行了一场激烈的意志之争，这场战斗最终我们都输了。
男孩教育的状况有些糟糕，我不禁怀疑，我在课堂上看到的模式可能与集体未能充分教育男孩有关。统计数字是严峻的。根据“接触男孩：教男孩：奏效的策略和原因”一书，男孩辍学的比率是女孩的两倍。男孩被幼儿园开除的频率几乎是女孩的五倍。男孩被诊断为学习障碍和注意力问题的比率几乎是女孩的四倍。他们做的家庭作业更少，在低分中得到的比例更大。男孩更有可能辍学，只占大学生的43%。此外，男孩被诊断为注意力缺陷和多动障碍(ADHD)的可能性几乎是女孩的三倍。考虑到11%的美国儿童--总共有640万--被诊断出患有多动症，那么在美国教室里蹦蹦跳跳的男孩可真不少。
“人力资源杂志”(Journal Of Human Resources)去年发表的一项研究证实了我的怀疑。似乎行为在老师的评分实践中扮演着重要的角色，因此，男孩从老师那里得到的分数比考试预测的要低。这项研究的作者得出结论，老师对行为的偏见，而不是对学习成绩的偏见，早在幼儿园就会惩罚男孩。平均而言，男孩从老师那里得到的行为评估分数较低，这些分数会影响教师对男孩智力和成就的整体看法。
虽然我喜欢教男孩，但我的许多同事不喜欢，特别是在荷尔蒙浸泡、精力充沛、心不在焉的初中和高中时期。教师和学校管理人员哀叹，男孩们太烦躁、太活跃、太捣乱，破坏了每个人的教育过程，同时破坏了他们自己的智力发展。
窥视大多数美国教室，你会看到一排排的课桌，老师们恳求学生留在座位上，不要与邻居交谈。良好行为的分数会奖励那些善于长时间坐着不动的学生。很多男孩没有这项技能。
迈克尔·赖切特博士和理查德·霍利博士与国际男校联盟(International Boys&#39；School Coalition)合作，在2009年发表了一项名为“教授男孩：有效实践的全球研究”的研究，试图找出对男孩教育真正有效的方法。这项研究观察了美国、英国、加拿大、澳大利亚、新西兰和南非的男孩，他们就读于规模不一的私立和公立学校，这些学校招收了来自不同种族和收入水平的广泛男孩。
作者们要求老师和学生清楚而客观地叙述一项教学活动，这种教学活动对提高男孩的学习能力尤其有效，或许不同寻常。总共2500份回复揭示了成功地教会了男孩的八种教学类别。最有效的经验教训包括以下几个要素中的一个以上：
产生最终产品的课程--例如，小册子、弹射器、诗歌或连环画。
那么，对男孩子来说，一堂很棒的课会是什么样子呢？到达男孩，教学男孩充满了例子，但这里有一个我想尝试下一次，我需要帮助我的学生复习信息，特别是大量的相关想法。把全班分成四人一组，分布在房间里。每队都需要纸和铅笔。在教室的前面，放置一份文档的副本，其中包括以某种图形形式教授的所有材料-例如，蜘蛛图。然后告诉学生，每组一人可以走到教室前面看文件30秒。当这30秒结束时，他们返回到他们的小组，写下他们记得的内容，试图完整地重新创建原始文档。学生们在整个过程中轮流进行，直到小组根据记忆将原始文档拼凑成一个团队。这些最终产品可能会由其他团队评分，作为最后的练习，每个学生都可以被要求回到自己的办公桌前，自己重新创建文档。
教育男孩的最有效的方法不是惩罚男孩相对更高的精力和竞争动力，而是利用他们的高能量、好奇心和对竞争的渴望。虽然Reichert和Hawley的研究是在全男生学校进行的，但这些课程可以在所有教室里使用，无论是男孩还是女孩。
教师们已经习惯了传统的课堂模式：整齐的教室由一排排顺从的学生组成。这是整洁和可预测的。但是，除非老师们停下来思考这些传统方法是否对女孩和男孩都有效，否则我们将继续给男孩教育大棒的短端。根据Reichert和Hawley的说法，所有孩子做得更好包括男孩做得更好。
无论不和谐、困惑和冲突可能在空气中徘徊，因为利益相关者断言关于男孩和女孩的性质和需求，以及他们之间在如何学习和应该被教授方面的本质或微不足道的差异的新的和相互竞争的主张，几乎没有人可以合理地驳斥这样一个命题，即许多男孩在学校不会茁壮成长。人们也不可能争辩说，没有理由或改进的余地。
教育工作者应该努力通过承认而不是忽视所有儿童的特殊和独特的教育需求来教育所有的儿童，无论是女孩还是男孩。正如马萨诸塞州贝尔蒙特·希尔学校(Belmont Hill School)校长理查德·梅尔文(Richard Melvoin)所写的那样，为女孩提供权利和机会很重要；呼吁减少男性，谴责他们的毒害，正如理查德·霍利(Richard Hawley)所说的那样，并没有很好地服务于男孩和女孩--或者男人和女人……。愿我们都能找到更好地理解这件复杂的作品的方法，这件作品叫做“人”。
我们想听听您对这篇文章的看法。向编辑提交一封信，或写信给letters@theatlantic.com。
是“大西洋月刊”的特约撰稿人和英语教师。她为“纽约时报”撰写“家长会”专栏，我是佛蒙特州公共广播电台的评论员，著有“失败的礼物：最好的父母如何学会放手，这样他们的孩子才能成功”(The Gift of Failure：How the Best Parents List to Left Go So They Children Can Success)一书。
note_en:
This year's end-of-year paper purge in my middle school office revealed a startling pattern in my teaching practices: I discipline boys far more often than I discipline girls. Flipping through the pink and yellow slips—my school's system for communicating errant behavior to students, advisors, and parents—I found that I gave out nearly twice as many of these warnings to boys than I did to girls, and of the slips I handed out to boys, all but one was for disruptive classroom behavior.
   The most frustrating moments I have had this year stemmed from these battles over—and for—my male students' attention. This spring, as the grass greened up on the soccer fields and the New Hampshire air finally rose above freezing, the boys and I engaged in a pitched battle of wills over their intellectual and emotional engagement in my Latin and English classes, a battle we  both lost in the end.
 Something is rotten in the state of boys' education, and I can't help but suspect that the pattern I have seen in my classroom may have something to do with a collective failure to adequately educate boys. The statistics are grim. According to the book   Reaching Boys, Teaching Boys: Strategies That Work and Why, boys are kept back in schools at twice the rate of girls. Boys get expelled from preschool nearly five times more often than girls. Boys are diagnosed with learning disorders and attention problems at nearly four times the rate of girls. They do less homework and get a greater proportion of the low grades. Boys are more likely to drop out of school, and make up only 43 percent of college students. Furthermore, boys are nearly three times as likely as girls to be diagnosed with Attention Deficit and Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD). Considering 11 percent of U.S. children—6.4 million in all—have been diagnosed with a ADHD, that's a  lot of boys bouncing around U.S. classrooms.
    A  study released last year in the Journal of Human Resources confirms my suspicions. It seems that behavior plays a significant role in teachers' grading practices, and consequently, boys receive lower grades from their teachers than testing would have predicted. The authors of this study conclude that teacher bias regarding behavior, rather than academic performance, penalizes boys as early as kindergarten. On average, boys receive lower behavioral assessment scores from teachers, and those scores affect teachers' overall perceptions of boys' intelligence and achievement.
  While I love teaching boys, many of my colleagues do not, particularly during the hormone-soaked, energetic, and distracted middle- and high-school years. Teachers and school administrators lament that boys are too fidgety, too hyperactive, too disruptive, derailing the educational process for everyone while sabotaging their own intellectual development.
 Peek into most American classrooms and you will see desks in rows, teachers pleading with students to stay in their seats and refrain from talking to their neighbors. Marks for good behavior are rewarded to the students who are proficient at sitting still for long periods of time. Many boys do not have this skill.
  In an attempt to get at what actually works for boys in education, Dr. Michael Reichert and  Dr. Richard Hawley, in partnership with the  International Boys' School Coalition, launched a study called   Teaching Boys: A Global Study of Effective Practices, published in 2009. The study looked at boys in the United States, United Kingdom, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, and South Africa, in schools of varying size, both private and public, that enroll a wide range of boys of disparate races and income levels.
 The authors asked teachers and students to "narrate clearly and objectively an instructional activity that is especially, perhaps unusually, effective in heightening boys' learning." The responses—2,500 in all—revealed eight categories of instruction that succeeded in teaching boys. The most effective lessons included more than one of these elements:
 Lessons that result in an end product—a booklet, a catapult, a poem, or a comic strip, for example.
  So what might a great lesson for boys look like?  Reaching Boys, Teaching Boys is full of examples, but here's one I want to try next time I need to help my students review information, particularly a mass of related ideas. Split the class into groups of four and spread them around the room. Each team will need paper and pencils. At the front of the room, place copies of a document including all of the material that has been taught in some sort of graphical form—a spider diagram, for example. Then tell the students that one person from each group may come up to the front of the classroom and look at the document for thirty seconds. When those thirty seconds are up, they return to their group and write down what they remember in an attempt to re-create the original document in its entirety. The students rotate through the process until the group has pieced the original document back together as a team, from memory. These end products may be "graded" by other teams, and as a final exercise, each student can be required to return to his desk and re-create the document on his own.
    Rather than penalize the boys' relatively higher energy and competitive drive, the most effective way to teach boys is to take advantage of that high energy, curiosity, and thirst for competition. While Reichert and Hawley's research was conducted in all-boys schools, these lessons can be used in all classrooms, with both boys and girls.
 Teachers have grown accustomed to the traditional classroom model: orderly classrooms made up of ruler-straight rows of compliant students. It's neat and predictable. But unless teachers stop to consider whether these traditional methods are working for both girls and boys, we will continue to give boys the short end of the educational stick. According to Reichert and Hawley, " Doing better by all children includes doing better by boys," and
 Whatever dissonance, confusion, and conflict may hover in the air as stakeholders assert new and competing claims about the nature and needs of boys and girls and the essential or trivial differences between them with respect to how they learn and should be taught, few could reasonably argue with the proposition that many boys are not thriving in school. Nor could one possibly argue there is no room to reason or improve.
 Educators should strive to teach all children, both girls  and boys by acknowledging, rather than dismissing, their particular and distinctive educational needs. As Richard Melvoin, headmaster at Belmont Hill School in Massachusetts,  wrote, "To provide rights and opportunities to girls is important; to call for the diminution of males, to decry their 'toxicity' as [Richard Hawley] has put it so poignantly, has not served boys and girls—or men and women—well ... May we all find ways of understanding even better this complex 'piece of work' called man."
  We want to hear what you think about this article.  Submit a letter to the editor or write to letters@theatlantic.com.
         is a contributing writer for  The Atlantic  and an English teacher. She writes   “The Parent-Teacher Conference”   column at   The New York Times , i s a commentator for  Vermont Public Radio , and is the author of    The Gift of Failure:   How the Best Parents Learn to Let Go So Their Children Can Succeed .
title: 停止惩罚男生在学校不能安静地坐着
title_en: Stop Penalizing Boys for Not Being Able to Sit Still at School

note:
据美国有线电视新闻网报道，在周三公布的一项新研究中，加拿大研究人员发现，在95名新冠肺炎危重患者中，84%的A型和AB型血型患者需要机械通气，相比之下，O型或B型血型患者的这一比例为61%。前者在重症监护病房停留的中位数为13.5天，而后者的中位数为9天。
Mypinder Sekhon博士是温哥华总医院的重症监护医生，也是这项研究的作者。他说，在治疗病人时，我脑海中一直浮现着血型，但我们需要在许多司法管辖区重复发现同样的情况，然后才能确定任何事情。
目前还不清楚这种可能的区别背后可能有什么原因；Sekhon说，一种解释可能是O型血的人不太容易凝血，这通常会导致更严重的病例。
无论哪种方式，Sekhon都不相信血型会取代其他严重的危险因素，比如年龄或合并症，他说人们的行为不应该因为他们的群体而不同。他说，如果其中一个人是A型血型，你不需要开始惊慌失措。如果你是O型血，你就不能去酒吧和酒吧。更多内容请访问CNN。
我们的目标是为用户创造一个安全和吸引人的地方，让他们在兴趣和激情上建立联系。为了改善我们的社区体验，我们暂停了文章评论。
note_en:
In a new study published Wednesday, researchers in Canada found that, among 95 critically ill COVID-19 patients, 84 percent of those with the blood types A and AB required mechanical ventilation compared to 61 percent of patients with type O or type B,  CNN reports. The former group also  remained in the intensive care unit for a median of 13.5 days, while the latter's median stay was nine days.
 Dr. Mypinder Sekhon, an intensive care physician at Vancouver General Hospital and the author of the study,  said blood type has been "at the back of my mind" when treating patients, but "we need repeated findings across many jurisdictions that show the same thing" before anything definitive is established.
 It's still unclear what may be behind the possible distinction; Sekhon  said one explanation could be that people with blood type O are less prone to blood clotting, which can often lead to more severe cases.
 Either way, Sekhon doesn't believe blood type will supersede other "risk factors of severity" like age or comorbidities, and he said people should not behave differently based on their group. "If one is blood group A, you don't need to start panicking," he  said. "And if you're blood group O, you're not free to go to the pubs and bars." Read more at  CNN.
 Our goal is to create a safe and engaging place for users to connect over interests and passions. In order to improve our community experience, we are temporarily suspending article commenting.
title: 新研究表明，血型可能影响新冠肺炎感染的严重程度
title_en: Blood Type May Affect Severity of COVID-19 Infection, New Study Suggests

note:
PassiveLogic是一家开发建筑控制平台的初创公司，今天筹集了1600万美元。一位发言人表示，所得资金将用于扩大PassiveLogic的员工队伍和加快产品研发。
商业建筑系统通常需要经过多年培训的工程团队。即使是用暖通空调这样的东西来翻新旧建筑也可能既昂贵又耗时，更不用说容易出错了。这就是为什么拥有硬件工程背景的企业家特洛伊·哈维(Troy Harvey)和杰里米·菲林吉姆(Jeremy Fillingim)在2016年联手创立PassiveLogic，目标是为价值超过1万亿美元的建筑市场带来自主控制。
PassiveLogic的Autonomy Studio平台使客户能够为建筑物和项目开发系统。表面上看，它通过在建筑物中提供基于物理定律的自我调节控制来简化自动化。(这与手动建筑系统所依赖的设置点和控制相反。)。PassiveLogic根据不断变化的建筑动态实时生成控制序列。该公司的决策引擎在应用控制行动之前会分析它们的含义，每秒分析数百万个序列期货，并在当前条件下只选择最优控制。
效用优化的人工智能是一个新兴的行业，竞争非常激烈。有Brainbox、Aquicore和Sidewalklabs的Mesa，它们的算法可以对暖通空调系统进行动态的细粒度调整。Auury是一家初创公司，开发连接到机器上的传感器，并记录数据，然后在云中进行分析。该公司与服务公司合作，诊断和优化工业HVAC等系统。GE Digital的Predex和初创公司Petasense提供类似的Wi-Fi、云和人工智能驱动的监控传感器。而Carbon Relay则拥有一款利用传感器数据来预测数据中心冷却使用情况的产品。
但PassiveLogic声称，它独一无二地使用了“数字孪生”技术来理解建筑物、设备、系统和乘员生理之间的物理和相互联系。在实践中，产品在以最佳效率为目标的同时对其操作进行自省，并生成自己的分析。与此同时，PassiveLogic使用基于“舒适性”指标的控制路径，而不是严格意义上的气温，这使得它可以评估乘客对给定的气候设置可能会有什么反应。
哈维说：“近年来，关于智能建筑的讨论很多，但当人们说‘智能’时，他们实际上只是指联网。”我们的数字孪生技术使建筑物真正实现了智能和自我管理，使它们超越了市场上的传统控制或其他人工智能云附加组件。“。
数字孪生模拟方法已经在其他工业领域流行起来。例如，总部位于伦敦的SenSat帮助建筑、采矿、能源和其他行业的客户为他们正在工作的项目创建位置模型，将现实世界转换为机器可以理解的版本。通用电气提供的技术允许公司对数字双胞胎进行建模，而不是实际的机器，并密切跟踪性能。Oracle提供的服务依赖于对象、设备和工作环境的虚拟表示。微软自己也提供了Azure Digital Twin和Project Bonsai，这两个软件都对模拟环境中的人、地点和设备之间的关系和交互进行了建模。
要开始使用PassiveLogic的平台，建筑物要安装该公司的蜂巢控制器，该控制器装有运行数字双引擎的12核处理器和神经核心。HIVE使用以太网和Wi-Fi，并具有内置网状路由，可帮助其设计控制系统、生成项目规格、创建物料清单，并将用户连接到当地的库存分销商。HIVE甚至可以即时修复布线错误，并根据基本建筑和系统布局自动生成控制拓扑和接口，它还可以与多个HIVE协同工作，自组装成一个网络。
PassiveLogic还销售传感器-Sarm和Spot Mini-它们可以测量气温、辐射温度、湿度和空气质量等，同时兼具恒温器的功能。两者都与蜂巢合作，通过蓝牙网格三角测量跟踪和响应乘客位置，监测活动以实时微调气候设置。用户可以通过浏览器或PassiveLogic的移动应用程序访问蜂窝屏幕上的设置。
Key Frame Capital and Adding共同领导PassiveLogic的A系列活动今天宣布，RET Ventures、A/O Proptech和NREP都参与其中。这使得PassiveLogic到目前为止的融资总额达到了近1800万美元，此前美国能源部和瑞士HVAC零部件制造商Blimo向这家初创公司授予了一份价值超过110万美元的合同，为下一代自动化系统定义行业数字孪生技术标准。
note_en:
PassiveLogic, a startup developing a building controls platform, today raised $16 million. A spokesperson says the proceeds will be put toward growing PassiveLogic’s workforce and accelerating product R&D.
 Commercial building systems often require engineering teams with years of training. Even retrofitting old buildings with things like HVAC can be costly and time-consuming, not to mention error-prone. That’s why Troy Harvey and Jeremy Fillingim, entrepreneurs with backgrounds in hardware engineering, teamed up in 2016 to found PassiveLogic with the goal of bringing autonomous controls to the over  $1 trillion building construction market.
  PassiveLogic’s Autonomy Studio platform enables customers to develop systems for buildings and projects. It ostensibly streamlines automation by providing self-adjusting controls in buildings based on the laws of physics. (That’s as opposed to the set-points and controls manual building systems rely on.) PassiveLogic generates control sequences in real time based on changing building dynamics. The company’s decision engine analyzes the implications of control actions before applying them, analyzing millions of sequence futures every second and selecting only the optimal control given the current conditions.
  Utility-optimizing AI is a burgeoning business, with plenty of competition. There’s  BrainBox,  Aquicore, and Sidewalklabs’  Mesa, whose algorithms make fine-grained adjustments to HVAC systems on the fly.  Augury, a startup developing sensors that attach to machines and record data that’s then analyzed in the cloud, works with service companies to diagnose and optimize systems like industrial HVAC. GE Digital’s Predix and startup Petasense offer similar Wi-Fi-enabled, cloud- and AI-driven monitoring sensors. And  Carbon Relay boasts a product that leverages sensor data to make predictions about datacenters’ cooling usage.
 But PassiveLogic claims it uniquely uses “digital twin” technology to understand the physics and interconnections among buildings, equipment, systems, and occupant physiology. In practice, the product introspects its operations while aiming for the best efficiency and generating its own analyses. At the same time, PassiveLogic uses control paths based on “comfort” metrics instead of strictly air temperature, allowing it to evaluate how occupants might react to a given set of climate settings.
 “There has been a lot of talk about smart buildings in recent years, but when people say ‘smart’ they really just mean connected,” Harvey said. “Our digital twin technology makes buildings truly intelligent and self-managing, taking them beyond conventional controls or other AI cloud add-ons in the marketplace.”
 Digital twin approaches to simulation have gained currency in other industrial domains. For instance, London-based  SenSat helps clients in construction, mining, energy, and other industries create models of locations for projects they’re working on, translating the real world into a version that can be understood by machines. GE offers technology that allows companies to model  digital twins of actual machines and closely track performance. Oracle has services that rely on virtual representations of objects, equipment, and work environments. And Microsoft itself provides  Azure Digital Twins and Project Bonsai, both of which model the relationships and interactions between people, places, and devices in simulated environments.
  To begin using PassiveLogic’s platform, buildings install the company’s Hive controller, which houses the 12-core processor and neural core that runs the digital twin engine. Hive works with Ethernet and Wi-Fi and has built-in mesh routing that helps it to design control systems, generate project specs, create bills of materials, and connect users to local stocking distributors. Hive even fixes wiring mistakes on the fly and automatically generates control topologies and interfaces from basic building and system layouts, and it works with multiple Hives in tandem to self-assemble into a network.
 PassiveLogic also sells sensors — Swarm and Swarm Mini —  that measure things like air temperature, radiant temperature, humidity, and air quality while doubling as thermostats. Both work with Hive to track and respond to occupant locations via Bluetooth mesh triangulation, monitoring activity to fine-tune climate settings in real time. Users can access settings on the Hive screen, from a browser, or with PassiveLogic’s mobile app.
 Keyframe Capital and Addition co-led PassiveLogic’s series A announced today with participation from RET Ventures, A/O Proptech, and NREP. It brings PassiveLogic’s total raised to date to nearly $18 million, and it comes after the U.S. Department of Energy and Swiss HVAC component manufacturer Belimo awarded the startup an over $1.1 million contract to define an industry digital twin technology standard for next-generation automation systems.
title: PassiveLogic的平台使没有编程专业知识的客户能够为建筑物和项目开发自主系统，该公司筹集了1600万美元的首轮融资
title_en: PassiveLogic, whose platform enables customers without programming expertise to develop autonomous systems for buildings and projects, raises $16M Series A

note:
来自五眼情报共享联盟国家的议员敦促科技公司开发后门，允许他们访问加密信息。
在一份公开声明中，七个国家表示，牢不可破的加密技术给公共安全带来了严重的风险。
虽然公民从额外的隐私中受益，但执法机构将端到端加密视为调查的障碍。
伦敦-来自五眼情报共享联盟国家的立法者警告科技公司，牢不可破的加密技术会给公共安全带来严重风险。
来自美国、英国、加拿大、澳大利亚和新西兰的部长们周日发表了一份声明，呼吁科技行业开发一种解决方案，使执法部门能够获取高度加密的信息。
这份由美国司法部长威廉·巴尔(William Barr)和英国内政大臣普里蒂·帕特尔(Priti Patel)签署的声明称，我们敦促业界解决我们的严重担忧，因为加密的应用方式完全排除了任何合法获取内容的机会。
这份发表在美国司法部网站上的声明还签署了印度和日本，这两个国家不是五只眼联盟的一部分。
苹果和Facebook等科技公司对用户的端到端通信进行加密，这意味着只有用户才能访问自己的消息。它适用于书面信息，以及音频和视频通信。
虽然公民从额外的隐私中受益，但执法机构将端到端加密视为调查的障碍，并一直呼吁科技公司引入后门，让执法机构能够进入。
我们呼吁科技公司与政府合作…。关于合理的、技术上可行的解决方案，政府说。
他们补充说，端到端加密对公共安全构成了重大挑战，包括对我们社会中像被性剥削的儿童这样高度脆弱的成员。
尽管这些国家确实承认，某些形式的加密在保护个人数据、隐私、知识产权、商业秘密和网络安全方面起着至关重要的作用。
最终，他们表示，他们希望与科技公司开发一种解决方案，使用户能够继续进行私人和安全的交流，同时也允许执法和科技公司监控犯罪活动。
去年，包括苹果、微软和WhatsApp在内的一批公司反对英国间谍机构GCHQ的一项提议，该提议将使间谍能够获取人们的加密信息。
根据这项建议，GCHQ建议在可疑的邮件帖子中添加“幽灵”收件人，这样发件人和收件人都不会注意到。
在去年5月发表的一封公开信中，科技公司和隐私团体表示，这样的功能将威胁基本人权。
title: “五只眼”警告科技公司加密带来“严重风险”
title_en: Five Eyes warns tech firms that encryption creates 'severe risks'
